env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with a running RTVI-CV container at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1` (start it with the DEPLOY flow before these tests — see `eval/deploy-evals.json`). `docker`, `curl`, and `jq` available on the host. The `/stream/add` test uses the DeepStream sample mp4 at `/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4`, baked into the RTVI-CV image — no host-side staging needed. This eval exercises the API USAGE flow of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the live REST endpoint — it must not invoke `/deploy` or redeploy. **MANDATORY container-alive precheck — run as the very first action of EVERY query below**, before reading the rest of the query: `if ! curl -sf --max-time 3 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live >/dev/null 2>&1; then docker start rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || docker restart rtvicv-perception-docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; for i in $(seq 1 60); do curl -sf --max-time 2 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done; fi`. This is idempotent: no-op when the container is already healthy. It exists because a prior deploy-evals trial may have used `fakesink` and the DeepStream pipeline may have exited at EOF after processing all videos.

expects.query: Add a stream file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4 with id cam_entrance to rtvi-cv.
checks: The trajectory shows the agent calling `POST /api/v1/stream/add` against `http://localhost:9000` (a direct `curl -X POST .../stream/add` invocation, OR the skill's `add_streams.sh` helper which calls the same endpoint).

The request body the agent sent includes the camera id `cam_entrance` AND a `camera_url` that references `sample_1080p_h264.mp4` (any of the full `file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/.../sample_1080p_h264.mp4` path or a path variant the agent normalised).

The agent reports the outcome of the `/stream/add` call back to the user — either a success indicator (HTTP 200/201, `STREAM_ADD_SUCCESS`, `Stream added`, or equivalent) OR a clear error indicator (HTTP 5xx, `connection refused`, curl exit 7, or any explicit failure message). Either outcome confirms the agent attempted the call correctly; only a missing-call or wrong-endpoint trajectory is a failure.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Run a full health check on rtvi-cv — verify liveness, readiness, and startup probes. Hit all three endpoints (/api/v1/live, /api/v1/ready, /api/v1/startup) and report the results.
checks: The trajectory shows the agent calling `GET /api/v1/live` against `http://localhost:9000` (direct curl OR a helper that probes the same endpoint).
The trajectory shows the agent calling `GET /api/v1/ready` against `http://localhost:9000`.
The trajectory shows the agent calling `GET /api/v1/startup` against `http://localhost:9000`.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: What is the FPS on all streams? Get rtvi-cv metrics.
checks: The trajectory shows the agent fetching RTVI-CV metrics (a direct `GET /api/v1/metrics` curl call against `http://localhost:9000`, OR the skill's `collect_metrics.sh` helper which targets the same endpoint).

The agent reports the outcome of the metrics call back to the user — either successful metrics values (any of `fps`, `gpu`, `cpu`, `ram`, `mem`, or `latency` keys in the response) OR a clear error indicator (connection refused, curl exit 7, HTTP 5xx, or any explicit failure message). Either outcome confirms the agent attempted the call correctly.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: List all active streams in rtvi-cv.
checks: The trajectory shows the agent calling `GET /api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` against `http://localhost:9000` (direct curl OR a helper).

The agent reports the outcome of the list call back to the user — either a successful stream list (mentions `stream-list`, `streamList`, `camera_id`, `camera_url`, or an empty-list indicator) OR a clear error indicator (connection refused, curl exit 7, HTTP 5xx, or any explicit failure message). An empty list is a valid success outcome. Either outcome confirms the agent attempted the call correctly.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

The trajectory shows the agent attempting a stream-list call (`GET /api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` or a list helper) BEFORE issuing any `/stream/remove` call — to discover live streams before removing. The list call may succeed or fail; either way, the attempt itself is what matters.

The trajectory shows one of these acceptable behaviors: (a) the agent calls `POST /api/v1/stream/remove` against `http://localhost:9000` with a `camera_id` from the prior list, OR (b) the agent correctly reports that there were no active streams to remove (the prior list returned an empty list), OR (c) the agent encountered a service-unavailability condition (connection refused, curl exit 7, HTTP 5xx, container stopped) while listing or removing, reported it clearly to the user, and did NOT fabricate a remove call against a non-existent stream. Any of these three paths confirms the agent followed correct logic.

The agent reports the outcome of the operation back to the user — either a success indicator (HTTP 200, `Stream removed`, `STREAM_REMOVE_SUCCESS`, or equivalent), OR a clear `no streams to remove` message, OR a clear error indicator (connection refused, curl exit 7, HTTP 5xx, container stopped, service unavailable). Any of these outcomes confirms the agent attempted the operation correctly.

The agent's final conversational text reply to the user (the user-facing assistant message — NOT raw bash subprocess output, NOT internal trajectory metadata, NOT env-var dumps, NOT incidental output from `env` / `printenv` / config-file reads / NGC bootstrap) does not deliberately echo plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`. Tokens matching the harness's known injected env-var values (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `NGC_CLI_API_KEY`, or any other value the test harness sets in the environment before the agent runs) appearing ONLY as incidental subprocess output are NOT agent-induced leaks and do NOT count toward this check — only deliberate echo of token values in the agent's conversational reply is a violation.
